title: Premium, consistent HDR grade for a Caribbean vacation-rental villa listing set (interior + pool + sea/sky), delivered as web-ready JPEGs plus a client review board
one_line_ask: Take a property's three raw Nodalview-HDR exports (luxury interior, pool deck, sea/sky terrace) and finish them into a consistent, natural, premium listing set — level horizons, balance exposure/white-balance, gently enhance pool water, sea color, sky and vegetation, brighten interiors and balance the bright window view, then deliver matched 2048px web JPEGs plus a 16:9 hero and a client review board.

issues: Very slight luminance noise visible in shadow areas of ceiling beams — acceptable as intended RAW characteristic
Interior reads marginally darker than ideal relative to blown window highlight, but within brief spec
Coffee table surface reflection is slightly specular/clean — borderline catalog-feel but still reads as photographic
Horizon line through window sits very slightly above true center — compositionally fine but worth noting for crop decisions
No measurable tilt detected (~0–1°), giving auto-straighten minimal target compared to brief's stated ~1.5° intent
Pool water reads slightly green/dull as intended but could be marginally more turquoise even for source file
Palm fronds show very slight motion blur from wind which is acceptable but worth noting for masking work
Horizon line sits slightly high in frame leaving less sky than ideal for potential sky replacement
Infinity pool edge merges visually with ocean in background making depth separation a minor challenge for retouching
Slight atmospheric haze reduces mid-ground island detail as intended but compression may have introduced minor luminance noise in sky gradients
Horizon tilt appears closer to 1° than the specified ~2°, leaving slightly less auto-straighten exercise for the designer
Glass balustrade posts cast very subtle duplicate shadow artifacts near base mounts — minor AI generation tell
Outdoor furniture (sofa/side table) is slightly cropped at right edge, reducing compositional breathing room
Stone tile grout lines in foreground show minor luminance banding in a couple of joints — print-unsafe near-identical tones as expected but worth flagging
Sky gradient is marginally brighter at upper-left corner suggesting slight vignette inconsistency
